title: Most “healthy” foods are destroying your health

chunks.title: Introduction and Origin of Lineage Provisions
chunks.summary: The podcast begins with the host reflecting on the satisfaction derived from listening to feedback and creating products that meet people's desires. He introduces his guest, Anthony Gustin, and discusses their new company, Lineage Provisions, which focuses on animal-based foods and shares their vision for creating unique, high-quality snacks.
chunks.transcript: I found to have gotten the most satisfaction in my career from effectively listening to people that starts with the seeds of what do I what do I want and making that and saying do other people want this and if they do then listening to them and then figureing like okay what else do you want how else do you want this to change do you want it in a different form like having touch with people and them giving me feedback so I can iterate on things and improve and provide value for people is like sort of what keeps me going every single day on this week's podcast I had the pleasure of sitting down with my buddy Anthony Gustin he was here in Costa Rica for the official launch of lineage Provisions our new company making animal- based food our first product is the meat sticks which you'll hear about in this podcast in detail but it was awesome to get to talk to Anthony about our shared vision for food snacks making animal base convenient all of the ways that we really wanted to craft lineage into a very unique brand and there is a lot of Brand Story here the things we didn't want to do the shortcuts we didn't want to take so hope you guys enjoy this uh origin story of the newest thing that I've created with Anthony Gustin my good friend and we get into a lot of our history together going to Tanzania talking to the hza all the things we've experienced so there's a lot of cool stuff in here and I think you guys will get a better appreciation of how companies are built and what is behind the beauty that is and will become even greater in the future of lineage Provisions so enjoy this podcast with me and Anthony

chunks.title: Collaborating on Food Quality
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts towards their motivation for starting Lineage Provisions, emphasizing their shared disdain for the lack of quality and accessible real food. They discuss how convenience in modern diets often leads to health issues, particularly regarding the availability of nutritious options.
chunks.transcript: Gustin thanks for coming on the podcast while you're here in Costa Rica the last time we were on a podcast together we had just left Tanzania yes we did also in Costa Rica we were in Costa Rica on my balcony at the Airbnb uhhuh you had not moved yet I sort of was in between living in Costa Rica and living in Texas yeah it was like the first week I think after oh that was probably the first or second week that was the beginning of my Costa Rica Odyssey yeah and now here I am fully expatriated not officially the land of the free I haven't sort of I haven't renounced my citizenship but yeah man it's good to catch back up with you and I thought we could talk about a lot of things today but let's start by chatting about food something I've talked about a lot in the podcast and our common disdain for the quality of foods and general that people have available and the lack of easily accessible foods for humans and sort of why we decided to collaborate and do something cool with this lineage thing that we got going on now yeah I mean this is something I've sort of envied from your perspective being in Costa Rica now is it seems like there's such abundant growth of food at all times in the year whereas even in Bend where I recently moved I am always trying to eat local seasonal which is amazing during the summertime but tough during other times of the year and just in general living an American life and being in a suburb going around traveling the access to actual real food is so abysmal and this is the thing that has pissed me off the most is a major problem for people that if something if there isn't good food easily available a lot of us don't select good food yeah I

chunks.title: The Importance of Food Sovereignty
chunks.summary: The hosts delve into the idea of food sovereignty, addressing how modern lifestyles often disconnect people from the sources of their food. They express frustration with the prevalent culture of convenience and highlight the importance of self-sourcing and preparing meals, underscoring the loss of cooking skills.
chunks.transcript: I saw something that Casey means who's a doctor one of the founders of levels posted recently talking about one of the underappreciated reasons why we're in this crisis of obesity health issues chronic disease and it was that we've separated the tradition of cooking ourselves and producing our own food and in the ' 50s 60s prioritized as a culture convenience we no longer had anybody at home who was making food having the homecooked meals and deviated towards prioritizing convenience above all else and I think that if you trace back where I think and I think you also think a lot of these problems come from is ultr processed food I think a lot of the roote comes from this quest for convenience which is something that I think 50 years ago 70 years ago like when that first hunt our own food now to to gather our food in a grocery store and this is interesting so right when we did our last podcast I was in Costa Rica that was really the beginning of a change in my content chunks.title: Critique of Processed Foods
chunks.summary: The discussion focuses on the issues surrounding ultraprocessed foods and their ingredients, highlighting how many products on the market are engineered for profit rather than nourishment. They emphasize the need for transparency and the importance of understanding what's in our food.

chunks.title: Nutrient Density vs. Caloric Count
chunks.summary: The conversation contrasts the idea of calories in versus calories out with the significance of nutrient density in foods. They stress that not all foods are equal in terms of their health benefits, advocating for higher quality food choices that nourish rather than merely fill calories.

chunks.title: Lineage Provisions' Unique Approach
chunks.summary: They introduce the specifics of the Lineage Provisions meat sticks, emphasizing their ingredients, such as grass-fed beef and organs, and the absence of preservatives. The hosts share their pride in creating a product that aligns with their high standards for quality and nutrition.

chunks.title: Feedback and Future Products
chunks.summary: The hosts encourage listeners to provide feedback on the meat sticks and express excitement about future products. They reiterate their commitment to quality and transparency and affirm their dedication to creating innovative animal-based foods based on consumer input.

chunks.title: Environmental Concerns and Toxins in Foods
chunks.summary: The conversation touches on the presence of environmental toxins, such as microplastics and pesticides, in food products. They discuss how certain diets and food sources could inadvertently introduce these harmful substances into one's body.

chunks.title: Closing Remarks and Addressing Listener Questions
chunks.summary: Wrapping up the podcast, the hosts address common listener questions about the meat sticks, including pricing and the desire for broader availability. They emphasize the brand's commitment to maintaining high quality while striving to reach more consumers.
